channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The Hawk Tuah girl remains radio silent after her crypto controversy
item.description: Has anyone checked in on Hawk Tuah girl? “When are we getting a new Talk Tuah episode? We’re starving for more Talk Tuah,” one X user posted yesterday. “Where is hawk tuah girl. Is she safe. Is she alright,” wrote another.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: The Paul Brothers' Reality TV Debut: "Paul American" Hits HBO Max
item.description: The Paul brothers, Jake and Logan, are trading punches for a family reality series 'Paul American' premiering on HBO Max March 27.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Texas governor orders ban on DeepSeek, RedNote for government devices
item.description: Texas ordered a ban on the Chinese AI startup DeepSeek on government devices days after the popular chatbot has shook investors and the tech community in recent weeks for its ability to compete with OpenAI.
item.dc:creator: Nadia Lathan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Apple reports dip in iPhone sales over the holidays, despite AI rollout
item.description: Apple on Thursday disclosed its iPhone sales dipped slightly during the holiday-season quarter, signaling a sluggish start to the trendsetting company’s effort to catch up to the rest of Big Tech in the race to bring artificial intelligence to the masses.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: How AI can drive business transformation
item.description: The race to implement artificial intelligence solutions across the enterprise is in full swing. Most are using AI to drive down bottom-line costs by doing things quicker or more frequently than they did before.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Trump vows sweeping tariffs on oil, pharmaceuticals and semiconductors
item.description: President Trump on Friday vowed to impose sweeping tariffs on semiconductor chips, pharmaceuticals, steel and aluminum and oil and gas. "We'll be doing pharmaceuticals. Importantly in drugs and medicines, etc. All forms of medicine and pharmaceuticals," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: NFL officials spotting the football isn't going away despite several technological advances
item.description: The NFL will engage its Competition Committee on technology to take virtual line-to-gain measurements next season, but officials will continue to spot the football.
item.dc:creator: Rob Maaddi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Google offers ‘voluntary exit’ for any US employees working on Pixel, Android
item.description: Google is asking its Platforms & Devices workers, including Android and Pixel, to consider resigning.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: I saw Nvidia's new DLSS 4 in action on the RTX 5090 and it's a true game changer – here's why
item.description: I've been critical of Nvidia in the past, but DLSS 4 has left me pleasantly surprised - and could be a strong factor for acceptable performance in future PC ports.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Most bosses admit using some form of snooping software to spy on staff
item.description: Bosses think remote employee monitoring is building trust, morale and productivity - but they’re wrong.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Ericsson provides 5G solutions to hybrid workforce
item.description: Founded as a telephony business in Stockholm in 1876, Ericsson is a provider of wireless and 5G services to telecoms operators worldwide and is currently investing heavily in cloud-based enterprise solutions. At the same time, the business’s basic principles remain unchanged.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: John Mulaney dunked on Meghan Markle and Prince Harry at star-studded Netflix event
item.description: The comedian poked fun at Meghan and Harry's Netflix track record, shortly after the duchess announced she was delaying the release of her lifestyle show in the wake of the Los Angeles wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: Why artificial intelligence could cause the lights to dim
item.description: "Data centers is one of the new industrial sectors that we've been looking at because it's part of the emerging economy and new technologies," said Arman Shehab The post Why artificial intelligence could cause the lights to dim first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: How DeepSeek will upend the AI industry
item.description: Chinese start-up DeepSeek’s cost-saving techniques for training and delivering generative AI (genAI) models could democratize the entire industry by lowering entry barriers for new AI companies.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump World learns how to deal with Musk
item.description: President Trump’s allies and aides are learning to deal with Tesla CEO Elon Musk, who has had a consistent presence in the early days of the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft reports 10% quarterly profit growth as it works to show AI investments paying off
item.description: Microsoft said Wednesday that its profit for the October-December quarter grew 10% as it works to capitalize on the huge amounts of money it has spent to advance its artificial intelligence technology.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: AI-powered automation set for gains in 2025
item.description: Automation technology has long been a part of IT’s arsenal, as enterprises work to simplify increasingly complex networking and operational environments. Looking ahead, the more AI is added to the automation mix, the more enterprise operations teams stand to gain.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Most in new poll disapprove of Musk's role in Trump administration
item.description: Most registered voters disapprove of tech billionaire Elon Musk’s role in the Trump administration, according to a poll released Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: General purpose AI could lead to array of new risks, experts say in report ahead of AI summit
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Advanced artificial intelligence systems have the potential to create extreme new risks, such as fueling widespread job losses, enabling terrorism or running amok, experts said in a first-of-its-kind international report Wednesday cataloging the range of dangers posed by the technology
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How Satya Nadella is weathering the storm of Trump’s first days
item.description: It’s going to be a long four years for Microsoft as the company tries to navigate the stormy chaos and pledges of retribution that follow wherever President Donald J. Trump’s mind wanders.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump’s 100% tariff threat on Taiwan chips raises cost, supply chain fears
item.description: President Donald Trump has suggested plans to impose tariffs of up to 100% on semiconductors imported from Taiwan, aiming to boost US manufacturing but risking higher costs and supply chain disruptions for tech firms and enterprises.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Doing authentication right
item.description: Any substantial website is going to need to customize itself for individual users. Thus, it is going to have to authenticate those users — that is, let them log in. As a software developer, it is your job to make the whole process as smooth and secure as possible.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Now US government agencies can use OpenAI’s ChatGPT too
item.description: Everyone wants to get their hands on AI tools, and employees in government agencies are no exception. Still, stakes are far higher for governments when it comes to security, privacy, and protecting the public.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: DeepSeek's new AI chatbot and ChatGPT answer sensitive questions about China differently
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Chinese tech startup DeepSeek ’s new artificial intelligence chatbot has sparked discussions about the competition between China and the U.S. in AI development, with many users flocking to test the rival of OpenAI's ChatGPT. DeepSeek’s AI assistant was the No.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Advancing Federal Workforce Efficiencies Amid Shifting Priorities
item.description: The new presidential administration has signaled a focus on finding efficiencies in and modernizing government, particularly with the creation of the Department of Government Efficiency.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Trump says Microsoft is one of the companies eyeing TikTok
item.description: President Donald Trump said Monday evening that Microsoft is among the U.S. companies looking to take control of TikTok to help the popular app avert an effective ban that could kick-in in April.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4
